title: Daily Market Digest: June 25, 2026
subtitle: A professional overview of today's market activities.

items.headline: Mixed Market Day Driven by AI Chip Optimism and Inflation Data
items.summary: U.S. equities are experiencing mixed trading today, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average and S&P 500 showing gains, while the Nasdaq Composite drifts slightly lower. Market sentiment was largely influenced by robust earnings from Micron Technology, which significantly boosted the semiconductor sector, and the release of in-line inflation data. However, ongoing concerns about elevated technology valuations and AI spending continue to temper enthusiasm across some tech names. Falling oil prices provided a tailwind for travel-related stocks but weighed on the energy sector.

items.headline: S&P 500 Performance
items.statContext: Intraday movement as of late morning.

items.headline: NASDAQ Composite Performance
items.statContext: Intraday movement as of late morning.

items.headline: Dow Jones Industrial Average Performance
items.statContext: Intraday movement as of late morning, up 419 points.

items.headline: Micron Technology Soars on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Micron Technology ($MU) surged 14% after reporting much stronger-than-expected profit and revenue for its latest quarter, alongside a robust growth forecast for the current quarter, driven by demand for memory chips in AI data centers.

items.headline: Bio-Techne Climbs
items.summary: Bio-Techne Corp. ($TECH) climbed 19.6% in today's trading.

items.headline: Wendy's Jumps on Meme Stock Revival
items.summary: Wendy's ($WEN) surged 25.7% today as retail traders appeared to revive the meme-stock playbook.

items.headline: Apple Drops After Price Hikes
items.summary: Apple ($AAPL) slumped 4.6% after the company raised prices for many of its products, including significant increases for Mac computers, making it the single heaviest weight on the S&P 500.

items.headline: Dollar Tree Loses Ground
items.summary: Dollar Tree ($DLTR) lost 3.6% in today's trading.

items.headline: PCE Inflation Rises, Core PCE Highest Since Oct 2023
items.summary: The Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index for May rose 0.4% month-over-month and 4.1% year-over-year, matching analyst expectations. Excluding food and energy, core PCE increased 0.3% monthly and 3.4% annually, marking its highest level since October 2023.

items.headline: Q1 GDP Revised Upward to 2.1%
items.summary: The U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA) revised its third estimate for first-quarter 2026 Gross Domestic Product (GDP) to an annual rate of 2.1%, a notable increase from the previous 1.6% estimate. This revision primarily reflects a downward adjustment to imports.

items.headline: May Durable Goods Orders Fall Less Than Expected
items.summary: New orders for manufactured durable goods decreased by 4.5% in May, a smaller contraction than the 4.7% expected by analysts. The decline was largely attributed to a drop in transportation orders, while orders excluding transportation rose by 1.3%.

items.headline: Industrials and Travel Lead, Energy and Tech Lag Amid Shifting Sentiment
items.summary: The Industrials sector showed strength today, with travel-related stocks also benefiting from falling crude oil prices, which dipped to pre-West Asia war levels. Conversely, the Energy, Technology, and Communication Services sectors experienced weakness, partly due to concerns over high tech valuations and declining oil prices impacting energy producers.

items.headline: Key Companies Report Earnings Today
items.summary: Several major companies released earnings today, June 25, 2026, including Commercial Metals Company ($CMC), Acuity Brands ($AYI), Darden Restaurants ($DRI), McCormick & Company ($MKC), and BlackBerry ($BB). Notably, Commercial Metals Company reported strong fiscal third-quarter results with a significant increase in adjusted earnings per share.
